steps.title: Expand the three terms
steps.content:
Expand the squared binomial and the two linear terms.
$$(2x+4)^2=4x^2+16x+16$$
$$-(x-3)=-x+3$$
$$2(x+5)=2x+10$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
Add the expanded pieces and combine matching terms.
$$4x^2+16x+16-x+3+2x+10$$
$$=4x^2+(16x-x+2x)+(16+3+10)$$
$$=4x^2+17x+29$$
The answer is A.
